name: Return To Golf
about: The Turn is a nonprofit organization focused on adaptive golf and fitness for individuals with physical disabilities. Located at the North Olmsted Golf Club, it offers a 15-acre campus with barrier-free access to an adaptive fitness center, indoor golf learning center, and a nine-hole accessible golf course. The facility is equipped with two SkyTrak Launch Monitor simulators and features specialized instruction from PGA professionals, physical therapists, and other trained staff. Programs are designed to improve physical function, build self-esteem, and foster community among participants.
why_golfers_like_it: Adaptive golf technology and equipment for players with disabilities
Year-round indoor and outdoor golf programs with professional instruction
Inclusive community focus fostering physical and emotional well-being
Barrier-free facility with accessible golf course and adaptive vehicles
Specialized programs for juniors and adults with diverse physical needs

food_and_drink.notes: No mention of on-site food service

simulator_hardware.notes: Two SkyTrak Launch Monitor simulators in the indoor learning center

faq.question: Do I need clubs?
faq.answer: The Turn provides adaptive golf equipment and specialized instruction for players with physical disabilities. Members can use the facility's adaptive golf vehicles and equipment.

faq.question: Can I bring food or drink?
faq.answer: There is no mention of outside food or drink policies in the provided content.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The facility features a nine-hole accessible golf course, with indoor simulator options available for additional practice and play.

faq.question: Is it kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, The Turn offers junior membership programs and youth instruction camps for children aged 8-13, including the PGA Jr League teams.
